name: Annette Funicello's Peanut Butter Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Corn Oil Margarine, softened (1 stick)
1/2 cup Skippy Creamy or Chunk Peanut Butter (or Trader Joe's Almond Butter)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
2. In a large bowl with mixer on low speed, beat together margarine, peanut butter, eggs, water, and vanilla until blended.
3. Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, beating until just combined.
4. Increase the speed to medium and beat for 2 minutes.
5. Optional: Stir in chocolate pieces into the batter.
6. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough about 3 inches apart onto ungreased cookie sheets.
7. Flatten slightly with the flat side of a glass.
8. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 10 to 12 minutes, or until lightly browned.
9. Cool on wire racks.
10. Store in a tightly covered container. Makes about 2-3 dozen cookies.
Peanut Butter Oat Cookies: Decrease flour to 1 cup, and add 1 cup quick oats. (Special thanks to Skippy Peanut Butter.) [The Great American Peanut Butter Book, by Larry & Honey Zisman, pg 18; St. Martin's Press, New York, 1985.]
